channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Rubio convenes countries on left-wing political violence
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday convened leaders from more than 60 countries to take part in the Trump administration's latest effort to quell what it calls "left wing" political terrorism.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday convened leaders from more than 60 countries to take part in the Trump administration's latest effort to quell what it calls "left wing" political terrorism.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Japan revises law to ensure supply of (male) heirs to the imperial throne
item.description: A popular princess drives support for having a female Japanese Emperor. But the country's first female prime minister opposes it.
item.content:encoded:
A popular princess drives support for having a female Japanese Emperor. But the country's first female prime minister opposes it.

(Image credit: Tomohiro Ohsumi)
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Boris Nadezhdin is a rare Russian politician speaking out against the Ukraine war
item.description: Boris Nadezhdin wants to run for Russia's parliament on a platform that gives voice to the Russians against the war in Ukraine. That's why, he says, the Kremlin is doing everything to stop him.
item.content:encoded: Boris Nadezhdin wants to run for Russia's parliament on a platform that gives voice to the Russians against the war in Ukraine. That's why, he says, the Kremlin is doing everything to stop him.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Hong Kong official warns booksellers on security risks after raids
item.description: The police operation marks the third round of arrests targeting independent bookstores in four months.
item.content:encoded:
The police operation marks the third round of arrests targeting independent bookstores in four months.

(Image credit: Kanis Leung)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine's tech-savvy defense minister in government reshuffle
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired the country's popular defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, who pushed for innovation on the battlefield through use of drones and turned the tables on Russia.
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired the country's popular defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, who pushed for innovation on the battlefield through use of drones and turned the tables on Russia.

(Image credit: ROMAN PILIPEY)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: The political risks from war in Iran. And, ICE's use of force is rising, report finds
item.description: The political implications the war in Iran is having on the GOP. And, a new report has found that ICE officers' use of force is not rare since President Trump took office for his second term.
item.content:encoded:
The political implications the war in Iran is having on the GOP. And, a new report has found that ICE officers' use of force is not rare since President Trump took office for his second term.

(Image credit: -/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: No Asian team managed to go far this World Cup (again). What's holding them back?
item.description: For the sixth World Cup in a row, no team from the Asian Football Confederation made it past the first knockout round, winning only three games out of 29. Asian teams are competitive — up to a point.
item.content:encoded:
For the sixth World Cup in a row, no team from the Asian Football Confederation made it past the first knockout round, winning only three games out of 29. Asian teams are competitive — up to a point.

(Image credit: Luke Hales)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Dismissal of Ukraine's popular defense minister draws intense criticism
item.description: As Ukraine signs drone deals and awaits licenses for Patriot missiles, the country's popular, tech-savvy defense minister, who helped Ukraine turn the tables on Russia through drone warfare, is fired.
item.content:encoded: As Ukraine signs drone deals and awaits licenses for Patriot missiles, the country's popular, tech-savvy defense minister, who helped Ukraine turn the tables on Russia through drone warfare, is fired.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Venezuela's earthquake recovery overshadows its push for democracy
item.description: After twin earthquakes killed thousands, Venezuela's recovery effort has pushed another crisis into the background: the fight for a return to democracy.
item.content:encoded:
After twin earthquakes killed thousands, Venezuela's recovery effort has pushed another crisis into the background: the fight for a return to democracy.

(Image credit: Ariana Cubillos)
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: The band playing when a Bangkok bar caught fire mourns its members among the 33 dead
item.description: Victims of this week's flash fire at a Bangkok music bar that took more than 30 lives included four of the six core members of the band playing when the blaze broke out.
item.content:encoded:
Victims of this week's flash fire at a Bangkok music bar that took more than 30 lives included four of the six core members of the band playing when the blaze broke out.

(Image credit: Sakchai Lalit)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
